chapter_info.subject: Science
chapter_info.chapter_name: A Journey through States of Water

points: Water exists in three states: Solid (Ice), Liquid (Water), and Gas (Water Vapour/Steam).
Ice has a fixed shape and doesn't flow; liquid water takes the shape of the container and flows; water vapour is invisible and fills all available space.
Water changes its state on heating or cooling: solid melts into liquid, liquid evaporates into gas, gas condenses into liquid, and liquid freezes into solid.
Evaporation is the conversion of liquid water into vapour. It speed depends on exposed surface area, temperature, wind movement, and humidity.
Condensation is the conversion of water vapour back into liquid state. This happens when vapour touches a cold surface.
Evaporation causes a cooling effect, which is utilized in traditional methods like earthen pots (matkas/surahis) and explains why fans make us feel cool by evaporating sweat.
Cloud Formation: Elevated water vapour cools and condenses around dust particles at high altitudes to form clouds.
The Water Cycle is the continuous circulation of water between the Earth's surface and atmosphere as rain, hail, or snow.
Shortage and Conservation: Only a small portion of Earth's water is fit for use. Shortages are rising with population, making it vital to use water wisely and avoid pollution.
